creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 1997-12-26 19:26 +0200, "Victor M. Grinenko" <vgrin@marion.iop.kiev.ua> wrote:
> >Synopsis:       how use RealTeck 8029: Plug and pray NE2000.

> >Release:        FreeBSD 2.1.5-STABLE i386

> >Description:
> pci0:1:2: Intel Corporation, device=0x7112, class=0x0c, subclass=0x03 int d irq 12 [no driver assigned]
>         map(20): io(d800)

First the easy answer: Upgrade to some 2.2 release,
there are modifications in the generic network code,
and the PCI NE2000 driver relies on them.


If you don't want to upgrade, then you can compile
in support for a simple ISA NE2000 card, but with
parameters reported by a verbose boot. But I need
the network card probe lines, not those from the
USB part of your chip set ...


Look for a vendor ID of 0x10ec and a device ID of
0x8029. You need to specify the port base and IRQ
in the config file, or may boot with "-c" and set
them in user config mode (if you compiled support
for it into your kernel).

Regards, STefan
